s2.ptxt: Remove the nut, washer, and control shaft lever.
Using a screwdriver, unstake the lock plate, and remove the lock nut and nut stopper.
Remove the 2 bolts and pull out the park/neutral position switch.
Remove the No. 2 breather plug from the transaxle case.
Remove the hose and O-ring from the No. 2 breather plug.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE OIL COOLER TUBE UNION (INLET OIL COOLER UNION)

s2.ptxt: Remove the union.
Remove the O-ring from the union.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE OIL COOLER TUBE UNION (OUTLET OIL COOLER UNION)

s2.ptxt: Remove the union.
Remove the O-ring from the union.
Remove the 2 bolts and the 2 speed sensors from the transaxle assembly.
Remove the 4 No. 1 transaxle case plugs from the transaxle case.
Remove the 4 O-rings from the 4 No. 1 transaxle case plugs.
Fix the transaxle assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE AUTOMATIC TRANSAXLE OIL PAN SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the drain plug and drain plug gasket from the oil pan.
Remove the 18 bolts, oil pan, and gasket.
Remove the 2 transmission oil cleaner magnets from the oil pan.
Remove the magnets and use them to collect any steel chips. Examine the chips and particles in the pan and on the magnet to determine what type of wear has occurred in the transaxle:
Disconnect the 7 connectors from the shift solenoid valves.
Remove the bolt, lock plate, and ATF temperature sensor.
Remove the bolt and transmission wire from the transaxle case.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE VALVE BODY OIL STRAINER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the 3 bolts and oil strainer.
Remove the O-ring from the oil strainer.
Support the valve body assembly and remove the 17 bolts and valve body assembly.
Remove the No. 1 governor apply gasket from the transaxle case.
Remove the transaxle case 2nd brake gasket from the transaxle case.
Remove the brake drum gasket from the transaxle case.
Remove the check ball body and spring from the transaxle case.
Remove the spring from the C-3 accumulator piston.
Using needle-nose pliers, remove the the C-3 accumulator piston from the transaxle case.
Remove the O-ring from the C-3 accumulator piston.
Apply compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi) to the oil hole and remove the reverse accumulator piston and spring.

item.ptxt: Applying compressed air may cause the piston to jump-out. When removing the piston, hold it by hand using a waste cloth.
Take care not to splash ATF when applying the air.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 O-rings from the reverse clutch accumulator piston.
Apply compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi) to the oil hole and remove the B-3 accumulator piston and 2 springs.

item.ptxt: Applying compressed air may cause the piston to jump-out. When removing the piston, hold it by hand using a waste cloth.
Take care not to splash ATF when applying the air.

s2.ptxt: Remove the O-ring from the B-3 accumulator piston.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE MANUAL VALVE LEVER SHAFT RETAINER SPRING

s2.ptxt: Using needle-nose pliers, remove the manual valve lever shaft retainer spring.
Remove the 2 bolts and manual detent spring sub-assembly.
Remove the 2 bolts and parking lock pawl bracket.
Using a screwdriver, slide the spacer.
Using a pin punch (φ3 mm) and hammer, drive out the pin.

atten4.ptxt: Slowly drive out the pin so that it will not fall into the transaxle case.

s2.ptxt: Remove the manual valve lever shaft, manual valve lever, and spacer.
Remove the parking lock rod sub-assembly from the manual valve lever sub-assembly.

atten4.ptxt: Align the dial with the notches on the manual valve lever sub-assembly to remove the parking lock rod sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE MANUAL VALVE LEVER SHAFT OIL SEAL

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, remove the oil seal from the transaxle case.
Fix the transaxle case with the oil pump side facing up.
Using a dial indicator, measure the input shaft end play.
Remove the 16 bolts.
Tap on the circumference of the transaxle housing with a plastic hammer to remove the transaxle housing from the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: The differential may be accidentally removed when the transaxle housing is removed.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 7 bolts and oil pump from the transaxle case.
Remove the thrust needle roller bearing from the underdrive planetary gear assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 2 THRUST BEARING UNDERDRIVE RACE

s2.ptxt: Remove the No. 2 thrust bearing underdrive race from the underdrive planetary gear assembly.
Remove the differential gear assembly from the transaxle case.
Remove the 2 overdrive brake gaskets from the transaxle case.
Remove the forward clutch assembly from the transaxle case.
Remove the thrust needle roller bearing from the forward clutch.
Remove the thrust needle roller bearing, multiple disc clutch hub, thrust needle roller bearing and No. 1 thrust bearing race from the transaxle case.
Using a dial indicator, measure the inside diameter of the multiple disc clutch hub bushing.
If the diameter is greather than the maximum, replace the multiple disc clutch hub with a new one.

atten3.ptxt: Check the contact surface of the bushing in the direct clutch shaft. If any scratch or discolor is identified, replace the direct clutch sub-assembly with a new one.

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt and pawl shaft clamp from the transaxle case.
Remove the parking lock pawl shaft from the transaxle case.
Push the parking lock pawl.

atten4.ptxt: Failure to do so will cause interference when the underdrive planetary gear is removed.

s2.ptxt: Remove the underdrive planetary gear assembly from the transaxle case.

atten3.ptxt: Do not drop the underdrive planetary gear assembly.

s2.ptxt: Remove the spring, pawl pin and parking lock pawl.
Remove the underdrive clutch assembly, thrust bearing, and bearing race from the transaxle case.
Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring from the transaxle case.
Remove the underdrive one-way clutch from the transaxle case.
Remove the outer race retainer from the one-way clutch.
Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring.
Remove the flange, 3 discs and 3 plates from the transaxle case.
Check if the sliding surfaces of the discs, plates and flange are worn or burnt.
If necessary, replace them.

item.ptxt: If the linings of the discs are peeled off or discolored, or even if a part of the groove is damaged, replace all discs.
Before assembling new discs, soak them in ATF for at least 15 minutes.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 11 bolts.
Tap on the circumference of the rear cover with a plastic hammer to remove the transaxle rear cover from the transaxle case.
Remove the 4 No. 1 transaxle case plugs from the transaxle rear cover.
Remove the 4 O-rings from the 4 No. 1 transaxle case plugs.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR CLUTCH OIL SEAL RING

s2.ptxt: Remove the 3 rear clutch oil seal rings from the transaxle rear cover.
Using SST, remove the needle roller bearing from the transaxle rear cover.
Using a screwdriver, remove the 3 apply gaskets.
Remove the bolt, clamp and brake apply tube.
Remove the clutch apply tube.
Remove the brake apply tube from the clamp.
Remove the thrust bearing and the direct clutch assembly from the transaxle case.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT PACK CLEARANCE OF DIRECT CLUTCH

s2.ptxt: Install the intermediate shaft and needle roller bearing on the transaxle rear cover.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the oil seal ring outers.

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, measure the direct clutch pack clearance while applying and releasing compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi).
If the pack clearance is not as specified, inspect the discs, plates and flange.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT PACK CLEARANCE OF OVERDRIVE CLUTCH

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, measure the overdrive clutch pack clearance while applying and releasing compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi).
If the pack clearance is not as specified, inspect the discs, plates and flange.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE DIRECT MULTIPLE DISC CLUTCH DISC

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring from the intermediate shaft.
Remove the flange, 3 discs, 3 plates and cushion plate from the intermediate shaft.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT DIRECT MULTIPLE DISC CLUTCH DISC

s2.ptxt: Check if the sliding surfaces of the discs, plates, and flange are worn or burnt. If necessary, replace them.

item.ptxt: If the lining of a disc comes off or discolors, or a groove is damaged, replace all discs.
Before assembling new discs, soak them in ATF for at least 15 minutes.

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring from the intermediate shaft.
Remove the flange, 3 discs and 3 plates from the intermediate shaft.
Check if the sliding surfaces of the discs, plates, and flange are worn or burnt. If necessary, replace them.

item.ptxt: If the lining of a disc comes off or discolors, or a groove is damaged, replace all discs.
Before assembling new discs, soak them in ATF for at least 15 minutes.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE OVERDRIVE CLUTCH RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Place SST on the clutch balancer and compress the spring with a press.
Using a snap ring expander, remove the snap ring from the direct clutch drum.

item.ptxt: Stop the press when the spring seat is lowered to the place 1 to 2 mm (0.039 to 0.078 in.) from the snap ring groove. This prevents the spring seat from being deformed.
Do not expand the snap ring excessively.

s2.ptxt: Remove the clutch balancer from the overdrive direct clutch drum.
Remove the overdrive clutch return spring from the overdrive direct clutch drum.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT OVERDRIVE CLUTCH RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Using vernier calipers, measure the free length of the spring together with the spring seat.
If the free length is shorter than the standard free length, replace the overdrive clutch return spring sub-assembly.
Install the intermediate shaft on the transaxle rear cover.
Holding the direct clutch piston by hand, apply compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi) to the transaxle rear cover to remove the direct clutch piston.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE OVERDRIVE DIRECT CLUTCH DRUM SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Holding the direct clutch drum by hand, apply compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi) to the transaxle rear cover to remove the direct clutch drum.
Using a screwdriver, remove the O-ring from the direct clutch drum.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE OVERDREVE DIRECT CLUTCH HUB SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the thrust bearing race, thrust bearing, and overdrive direct clutch hub from the planetary gear assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE REAR PLANETARY SUN GEAR ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Remove the rear planetary sun gear assembly from the transaxle case.
Remove the thrust needle roller bearing and thrust bearing race from the rear planetary sun gear assembly.
Remove the No. 2 planetary carrier thrust washer from the rear planetary sun gear assembly.
Remove the one-way clutch assembly and the thrust needle roller bearing from the transaxle case.
Remove the one-way clutch inner race from the one-way clutch assembly.
Remove the one-way clutch sleeve from the transaxle case.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE NO. 1 PLANETARY CARRIER THRUST WASHER

s2.ptxt: Remove the No. 1 planetary carrier thrust washer from the planetary gear assembly.
Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring.
Remove the flange, 3 discs and 3 plates from the transaxle case.
Check if the sliding surfaces of the discs, plates and flange are worn or burnt.
If necessary, replace them.

item.ptxt: If the linings of the discs are peeled off or discolored, or even if a part of the groove is damaged, replace all discs.
Before assembling new discs, soak them in ATF for at least 15 minutes.

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring.
Remove the second brake piston assembly from the transaxle case.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE 2ND BRAKE PISTON RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Place SST on the return spring and compress it with a press.
Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring.
Remove the 2nd brake piston return spring sub-assembly.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT 2ND BRAKE PISTON RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Using vernier calipers, measure the free length of the spring together with the spring seat.
If the free length is shorter than the standard free length, replace the 2nd brake piston return spring sub-assembly.
Hold the 2nd brake piston and apply compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi) to the 2nd brake cylinder to remove the 2nd brake piston.
Remove the 2 O-rings from the 2nd brake piston.
Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring.
Remove the rear planetary gear assembly from the transaxle case.
Remove the 2 thrust needle roller bearings, No. 2 thrust bearing races, No. 3 thrust bearing race, and input sun gear from the transaxle case.
Remove the flange, 5 discs and 5 plates from the transaxle case.
Check if the sliding surfaces of the discs, plates and flange are worn or burnt.
If necessary, replace them.

item.ptxt: If the linings of the discs are peeled off or discolored, or even if a part of the groove is damaged, replace all discs.
Before assembling new discs, soak them in ATF for at least 15 minutes.

s2.ptxt: Using a chisel and hammer, unstake the lock washer.

atten3.ptxt: Push down all claws of the washer. Otherwise SST cannot be fully pressed against the nut, and the nut cannot be loosened.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, remove the nut and washer.
Using SST and a press, remove the front planetary gear assembly from the counter drive gear.
Remove the front planetary gear assembly from the brake hub.
Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring and front planetary ring gear from the brake hub.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE 1ST AND REVERSE BRAKE RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Place SST on the return spring, and compress the return spring with a press.
Using a snap ring expander, remove the snap ring.

item.ptxt: Stop the press when the spring seat is lowered to the place 1 to 2 mm (0.039 to 0.078 in.) from the snap ring groove to prevent the spring seat from being deformed.
Do not expand the snap ring excessively.

s2.ptxt: Remove the return spring.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT 1ST AND REVERSE BRAKE RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Using vernier calipers, measure the free length of the spring together with the spring seat.
If the free length is shorter than the standard free length, replace the 1st and reverse brake return spring sub-assembly.
Apply compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi) to the transaxle case to remove the 1st and reverse brake piston.

item.ptxt: Applying compressed air may cause the piston to jump-out. When removing the piston, hold it by hand using a waste cloth.
Take care not to splash ATF when applying the air.

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 O-rings from the 1st and reverse brake piston.
Using SST and a press, remove the counter drive gear from the transaxle case.
Tighten the 2 bolts evenly and make clearance of approx. 20.0 mm (0.797 in.) between the counter drive gear and the inner race.
Using SST, remove the tapered roller bearing inner race.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE COUNTER DRIVE ANGULAR BALL BEARING

s2.ptxt: Remove the bearing inner race (rear side) and angular ball (rear side).
Using SST, remove the bearing outer race (rear side) from the transaxle case.
Remove the angular ball (front side).
Using SST and a press, remove the bearing outer race (front side) from the transaxle case.
Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring from the transaxle case.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE UNDERDRIVE BRAKE RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Place SST on the return spring, and compress the return spring with a press.
Using a snap ring expander, remove the snap ring.

item.ptxt: Stop the press when the spring seat is lowered to the place 1 to 2 mm (0.039 to 0.078 in.) from the snap ring groove to prevent the spring seat from being deformed.
Do not expand the snap ring excessively.

s2.ptxt: Remove the return spring.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT UNDERDRIVE BRAKE RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Using vernier calipers, measure the free length of the spring together with the spring seat.
If the free length is shorter than the standard free length, replace the under drive brake return spring sub-assembly.
Apply compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi) to the transaxle case to remove the underdrive brake piston.
Remove the 2 O-rings from the underdrive brake piston.
Using SST, remove the needle roller bearing from the transaxle case.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE UNDERDRIVE CLUTCH DRUM OIL SEAL RING

s2.ptxt: Remove the 2 oil seal rings from the transaxle case.
Remove the 2 No. 1 transaxle case plugs.
Remove the 2 O-rings from the 2 No. 1 transaxle case plugs.
Using SST, remove the underdrive cylindrical roller bearing from the transaxle case.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE UNDERDRIVE OUTPUT SHAFT OIL SEAL RING

s2.ptxt: Remove the oil seal ring from the transaxle housing.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE DIFFERENTIAL GEAR LUBE APPLY TUBE

s2.ptxt: Remove the bolt, transaxle apply tube clamp and differential gear lube apply tube from the transaxle housing.
